rationale: The citing paper does more than list this reference in passing: it explicitly credits Kivetz, Urminsky, and Zheng (2006) for showing points-pressure/goal-gradient purchase acceleration in single loyalty programs. It then reuses that construct in its own analysis by defining point-distance variables and noting they quantify points-pressure effects. This citation helps frame the psychological mechanism behind loyalty behavior and informs how the citing paper interprets deviations from optimal decisions in competing-program settings.
relation_description: The citing paper treats Kivetz et al. (2006) as prior conceptual evidence on goal-gradient and points-pressure effects in loyalty programs, then uses that idea to structure and interpret its own dynamic-choice analysis with competing programs. In this sense, the cited study provides a key behavioral foundation that the newer paper carries into a broader competitive context.
